signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD/CHF at 0.7165 (implied) — NZD is the week's G10 leader (+1.480%) while CHF laggard status (-0.739%) creates a 2.218% spread. Despite NZD COT LF net short -25,757 (19.5% long), the RBNZ hiking bias and +250bp OIS carry advantage over SNB drive the bilateral divergence. NZD/USD recovery faces overhead SMAs, but the NZD/CHF cross remains the primary expression of G10 strength vs weakness. RBNZ 2.5%.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.36 — Price holds above the 160.00 psychological level previously associated with BoJ intervention warnings. JPY COT LF net short -137,828 (25.3% long) remains crowded, while the 25d RR at -1.38 (moderate put skew) signals the options market is paying for downside protection against a carry unwind. IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 4.0% creates a +3.2% VRP, pricing in elevated event risk. Japan's urge for pension funds to invest domestically provides a fundamental catalyst for JPY repatriation, threatening the +275bp USD carry advantage.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility + Positioning Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6967 — Options market pricing reflects extreme caution with IV at 30.7% vs HV30 of 7.0%, a +23.7% VRP. This massive vol premium coincides with a 25d RR of -0.86 (put skew), suggesting traders are hedging for a sharp reversal despite AUD COT LF net long +21,597 (62.1% long). The -60bp carry deficit vs USD and Middle East tensions cap upside potential; a break of the weekly trend would trigger a liquidation of the spec long position.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + CESI Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4122 — The pair's correlation with WTI Oil has collapsed to -0.893 (z = -2.00σ) vs the 252d norm of +0.006, indicating CAD is no longer trading as a pure petro-currency. CAD weakness is instead driven by deteriorating fundamentals, with a negative CESI (-9 misses) vs USD's strong positive CESI (+278 beats). CAD COT LF net short -92,861 (20.6% long) is approaching extreme levels, but the +150bp USD carry advantage and superior US data flow support the current trend. Watch the BoC meeting on 15 Jul for a shift in hold bias.
